hero.title: 5 Days, Varanasi & Ayodhya.
hero.titleAccent: The Sacred Ganges and the Birthplace of Rama.
hero.intro: Two of the holiest cities of the north in one journey. Varanasi's ghats and Ganga Aarti, and Ayodhya's Ram Mandir and Sarayu river. A deep spiritual circuit through the heart of sacred India.
hero.heroImageAlt: Varanasi ghats and Ayodhya Ram Mandir pilgrimage

overview.heading: 5 Days Best of Varanasi + Ayodhya: The Full Picture
body: This 5 day spiritual circuit links the two great sacred cities of the north. Varanasi, the oldest living city, where the ghats and the Ganga Aarti embody the cycle of life and death on the Ganges. And Ayodhya, the birthplace of Lord Rama, where the grand new Ram Mandir has drawn pilgrims from across the world.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip comes with a professional guide and a private vehicle linking the two cities. You experience the sunrise boats and aarti of Varanasi and the temples and Sarayu river of Ayodhya, a profound journey through the spiritual heart of India.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Varanasi's Ghats & Ganga Aarti
items.body: The sunrise boat ride along the ghats and the spectacular evening Ganga Aarti, the essential experiences of the spiritual capital.

items.title: Ayodhya's Ram Mandir
items.body: The magnificent new Ram Janmabhoomi temple, built on the believed birthplace of Lord Rama, the spiritual heart of resurgent Ayodhya.

items.title: Sarayu River & Sacred Sites
items.body: Hanuman Garhi, the serene Guptar Ghat and the Sarayu river aarti at Ayodhya, alongside Sarnath near Varanasi, deepen the pilgrimage.

days.title: Arrive Varanasi
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening Ganga Aarti from a boat.
suggestedActivities: Evening Ganga Aarti from a boat

days.title: Sunrise Boat, Temples & Sarnath
days.description: Sunrise boat ride, temples and Sarnath.

days.title: Varanasi to Ayodhya

days.title: Ayodhya Sightseeing

days.title: Depart
days.description: A final darshan before your transfer to the airport.

whyBook.heading: Two Sacred Cities, One Guided Journey
whyBook.body: Linking Varanasi and Ayodhya takes good routing and a guide who understands both traditions. We handle the boats, the timings and the temples. Dhesu has arranged spiritual India journeys since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Varanasi & Ayodhya Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 5 day spiritual circuit links the two great sacred cities of the north: Varanasi on the Ganges and Ayodhya, the birthplace of Lord Rama.

With a professional guide and a private vehicle, you experience the sunrise boats and aarti of Varanasi and the Ram Mandir and Sarayu river of Ayodhya, a profound and well-paced pilgrimage.

For the full circuit including Prayagraj, see our 6 day Sacred Triangle package.

sections.heading: A Deeper Spiritual Circuit
sections.body:
Varanasi and Ayodhya together form one of the most meaningful pilgrimages in North India. Varanasi embodies the eternal cycle of life and death on the Ganges; Ayodhya celebrates the divine through the story of Lord Rama.

Visiting both, with the new Ram Mandir as a highlight, gives a rich sense of the spiritual landscape of the Ganges plains.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Varanasi or Ayodhya Airport (VNS / AYJ). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time for this pilgrimage?
items.answer: October to March offers the most comfortable weather. Festivals like Dev Deepawali at Varanasi and Ram Navami or Deepotsav at Ayodhya are spectacular but very crowded. Winter is ideal for the boats and temple visits.

items.question: How far is Ayodhya from Varanasi?
items.answer: Ayodhya is around 5 to 6 hours by road from Varanasi. The private vehicle makes the journey comfortable, with stops along the way. Both cities also have airports for those who prefer to fly between legs of a larger trip.

items.question: Can I add Prayagraj to this trip?
items.answer: Yes. Adding Prayagraj, with its Triveni Sangam where three rivers meet, completes the sacred circuit. Our 6 day Sacred Triangle package combines Varanasi, Ayodhya and Prayagraj.
